_comment: Copy to mcp.json and fill in values. Required servers are needed for the demo; optional ones are nice-to-have.

databricks.description: Query Unity Catalog, run SQL, manage jobs

github.description: Create PRs, manage repos, track issues

confluence.description: Read and write Confluence pages for documentation

context7.description: Get up-to-date library documentation (Delta Lake, PySpark, XGBoost, MLflow)

brave-search.description: Search for best practices and research

memory.description: Persist learnings and facts across sessions

obsidian.description: Document pipelines and experiments in your vault

databricks: 1. Install Databricks CLI: pip install databricks-cli
3. The managed MCP uses your Databricks CLI auth automatically
github: 1. Create a Personal Access Token (PAT) in GitHub Settings
2. Grant: repo (Full control of private repositories)
confluence: 3. Update CONFLUENCE_SPACE to your space key

databricks: List tables: 'Show me all tables in my_catalog.grocery'
Query data: 'Query the top 10 products by order count'
Check job: 'What's the status of my last job run?'
github: Create PR: 'Create a pull request for my changes'
Create issue: 'Create an issue for adding data quality checks'
confluence: Document: 'Create a Confluence page documenting my pipeline'
Search: 'Find Confluence pages about data quality'
